---
title: "exposure_bias"
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)

d <- readRDS("_targets/objects/d_gridded") %>% dplyr::select(-geometry)

#d2 <- read.csv("data/records_with_midtone_share.csv")

#d <- merge(d,d2)
```

```{r}

hist(d$illum_index)
```

```{r}

cor(d$illum_index,d$hls_ch1_below0.2)
cor(d$latitude,d$hls_ch1_below0.2)
```

```{r}

m <- lm(illum_index ~ hls_ch1_below0.2, data = d)

# extract residuals
d$resid <- residuals(m)

# correlate residuals with latitude
cor_resid_lat <- cor.test(d$resid, d$latitude, use = "complete.obs")

cor_resid_lat
```

```{r}

library(lmtest)
library(sandwich)

m <- lm(illum_index ~ hls_ch1_below0.2, data = d)
d$resid <- resid(m)


# test residual ~ latitude with species-clustered SEs

m_lat <- lm(resid ~ latitude, data = d)
summary(m_lat)

m_dark <- lm(hls_ch1_below0.2 ~ latitude, data=d)
summary(m_dark)

# use species identity
coeftest(m_lat, vcov = vcovCL(m_lat, cluster = d$species))
```

```{r}

# Find the median illum_index
median_illum <- median(d$illum_index, na.rm = TRUE)

# Filter to the top 50% of illum_index values
d_top50 <- d %>% 
  filter(illum_index >= median_illum)

# Correlation between illum_index and hls_ch1_below0.2 within top 50%
cor_top50 <- cor(d_top50$illum_index, d_top50$hls_ch1_below0.2, use = "complete.obs")
cor_top50


cor.test(d_top50$illum_index,d_top50$latitude)
cor.test(d_top50$hls_ch1_below0.2,d_top50$latitude)
```

```{r}

m_full <- lm(hls_ch1_below0.2 ~ latitude + illum_index, data = d)
summary(m_full)

m_simple <- lm(hls_ch1_below0.2 ~ latitude, data = d)
summary(m_simple)
```

```{r}

cor.test(d$hls_ch1_below0.2, d$latitude)
m_full <- lm(hls_ch1_below0.2 ~ latitude + illum_index, data = d)
library(ppcor)
pcor.test(d$hls_ch1_below0.2, d$latitude, d$illum_index)


```

```{r}

cor(d$exposure_bias_median,d$hls_ch1_below0.2)
cor(d$illum_index,d$hls_ch1_below0.2)

cor(d$latitude,d$hls_ch1_below0.2)
```

```{r}

summary(lm(illum_index ~ hls_ch1_below0.2, data = d))$r.squared

m0 <- lm(hls_ch1_below0.2 ~ latitude, data = d)                     # no correction
m1 <- lm(hls_ch1_below0.2 ~ latitude + I(0.25 * illum_index), data = d)  # weak correction
m2 <- lm(hls_ch1_below0.2 ~ latitude + I(0.5 * illum_index), data = d)   # medium correction
m3 <- lm(hls_ch1_below0.2 ~ latitude + illum_index, data = d)            # full correction

summary(m0)
summary(m1)
summary(m2)
summary(m3)
```

```{r}

library(dplyr)

d %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarise(r = cor(illum_index, hls_ch1_below0.2, use="complete.obs")) %>%
  summarise(mean_r = mean(r, na.rm=TRUE))
```

```{r}
library(lavaan)

model <- '
  S =~ 1*hls_ch1_below0.2
  L =~ 1*illum_index

  # Allow darkness and illum correlation
  S ~~ L

  # Allow indicators residuals to correlate too
  hls_ch1_below0.2 ~~ illum_index

  # Latitude → true darkness
  S ~ latitude
'

fit <- sem(model, data = d)
summary(fit, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE)


```

```{r}

cor(d$midtone_share,d$latitude)
cor(d$midtone_share,d$hls_ch1_below0.2)
```

```{r}

d <- d %>%
  group_by(species, cell) %>%
  mutate(
    illum_dev_sc = mean_cielab_lightness - mean(mean_cielab_lightness, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

cor(d$illum_dev_sc, d$hls_ch1_below0.2, use="complete.obs")
```

```{r}

library(dplyr)

d <- d %>%
  group_by(species, cell) %>%
  mutate(
    illum_dev_sc = hls_ch1_below0.2 - median(hls_ch1_below0.2, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

cor(d$illum_dev_sc, d$latitude, use="complete.obs")
```

```{r}

d <- d %>%
  group_by(species, cell) %>%
  mutate(
    illum_dev_sc = (mean_cielab_lightness - mean(mean_cielab_lightness, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                   mean(mean_cielab_lightness, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


cor(d$illum_dev_sc, d$latitude, use="complete.obs")
cor(d$illum_dev_sc, d$hls_ch1_below0.2, use="complete.obs")
cor(d$hls_ch1_below0.2, d$latitude, use="complete.obs")



library(ppcor)

pcor.test(
  d$hls_ch1_below0.2,
  d$latitude,
  d$illum_dev_sc,
  method = "pearson"
)

dt <- d %>% select(illum_dev_sc,img_url)
d$img_url
```

sd of illum ptterns

untify skew nd devition

kurtosis

zoom wed 2

I want to test whether illumination is a confounder for my observed effect of latitude and climate on dark color. The most obvious way is including it in my models. I am trying to quantify illum by chalking up all variation WITHIN a species-cell

```{r}

cor.test(d_sp_cell$srad,d_sp_cell$tmean)
cor.test(d_sp_cell$vp,d_sp_cell$tmean)
```

```{r}

library(dplyr)

# make a unique ID for species×cell
d <- d %>%
  mutate(sc_id = paste(species, cell, sep = "_"))


# list available groups
unique_sc <- unique(d$sc_id)

# pick the 1st and 20th
sc1  <- unique_sc[1]
sc20 <- unique_sc[20]

d_sc1 <- d %>% filter(sc_id == sc1)
d_sc20 <- d %>% filter(sc_id == sc20)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(d_sc20, aes(x = illum_dev_sc)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05, color = "black", fill = "skyblue") +
  labs(
    title = paste("Histogram of illum_dev_sc for", sc1),
    x = "illum_dev_sc",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

library(solartime)

# Assuming your datetime vector is stored in d$datetime
# and you have a mean_cielab_lightness column in your dataframe d

# First, let's convert the datetime to POSIXct if it isn't already
d$datetime <- as.POSIXct(d$datetime, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tz = "UTC")

d$local_time <- solartime::getSolarTimeHour(d$datetime,d$longitude)

# Test correlation between local_time_hour and mean_cielab_lightness
correlation_result <- cor(d$local_time, d$mean_cielab_lightness, 
                         use = "complete.obs")

cor_test <- cor.test(d$local_time, d$mean_cielab_lightness)
print(cor_test)
```

```{r}

library(colorEvoHelpers)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(phyr)
library(dplyr)
library(car)
library(performance)
library(MuMIn)
library(buildmer)
library(glmmTMB)
library(splines)
library(sjPlot)
library(ggeffects)
library(brms)
library(sjPlot)

d <- readRDS("_targets/objects/d_sp_cell")
```
